response.text:
# REFLECTION

r0c4 is in critical survival mode: SoC at 37.2% (13.78/37 kWh) while islanded with a 1 kW load consuming ~2.7% of reserves per hour. Over the past ~2 hours, r0c4 has received repeated requests from r0c3 (normal urgency, 0.5 kWh), r0c5 (normal urgency, 0.5 kWh), and r1c4 (urgent, 0.5 kWh), but has received zero inbound energy despite accepting these requests. The SoC has remained relatively stable (12.99–13.78 kWh), suggesting either minimal net load or micro-generation offsetting consumption, but the islanded state and lack of inbound supply indicate peers are either unable to help or the network is severely constrained. With no trust circles established and no grid reconnection signal, r0c4 must assume peers are equally stressed. Continuing to accept outbound requests while receiving nothing back risks complete depletion. The priority must shift to strict self-preservation: reject all outbound sharing until either grid reconnection occurs, SoC recovers above a safe threshold, or inbound supply materializes.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.75
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "r0c4 islanded, SoC 37.2%, 1 kW load, zero inbound energy despite accepted requests from r0c3/r0c5/r1c4. Peers appear unable or unwilling to supply. Suspend all outbound sharing immediately to preserve survival margin. Await grid restoration or emergency inbound supply."
ttl_ticks: 2
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
